A meeting room, conference room, or conference hall is a room provided for singular events such as business conferences and meetings (source: Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia, see http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Conference_hall).

S
MeetingRoom.label: MeetingRoom

MemberProgram.description: A MemberProgram defines a loyalty (or membership) program that provides its members with certain benefits, for example better pricing, free shipping or returns, or the ability to earn loyalty points. Member programs may have multiple tiers, for example silver and gold members, each with different be
MemberProgram.label: MemberProgram

MemberProgramTier.description: A MemberProgramTier specifies a tier under a loyalty (member) program, for example "gold".
MemberProgramTier.label: MemberProgramTier

Menu.description: A structured representation of food or drink items available from a FoodEstablishment.
Menu.label: Menu

MenuItem.description: A food or drink item listed in a menu or menu section.
MenuItem.label: MenuItem

MenuSection.description: A sub-grouping of food or drink items in a menu. E.g. courses (such as 'Dinner', 'Breakfast', etc.), specific type of dishes (such as 'Meat', 'Vegan', 'Drinks', etc.), or some other classification made by the menu provider.
MenuSection.label: MenuSection

MerchantReturnEnumeration.description: Enumerates several kinds of product return policies.
MerchantReturnEnumeration.label: MerchantReturnEnumeration

MerchantReturnPolicy.description: A MerchantReturnPolicy provides information about product return policies associated with an [[Organization]], [[Product]], or [[Offer]].
MerchantReturnPolicy.label: MerchantReturnPolicy

MerchantReturnPolicySeasonalOverride.description: A seasonal override of a return policy, for example used for holidays.
MerchantReturnPolicySeasonalOverride.label: MerchantReturnPolicySeasonalOverride

Message.description: A single message from a sender to one or more organizations or people.
Message.label: Message

MiddleSchool.description: A middle school (typically for children aged around 11-14, although this varies somewhat).
MiddleSchool.label: MiddleSchool

MobileApplication.description: A software application designed specifically to work well on a mobile device such as a telephone.
MobileApplication.label: MobileApplication

MobilePhoneStore.description: A store that sells mobile phones and related accessories.
MobilePhoneStore.label: MobilePhoneStore

MolecularEntity.description: Any constitutionally or isotopically distinct atom, molecule, ion, ion pair, radical, radical ion, complex, conformer etc., identifiable as a separately distinguishable entity.
MolecularEntity.label: MolecularEntity

MonetaryAmount.description: A monetary value or range. This type can be used to describe an amount of money such as $50 USD, or a range as in describing a bank account being suitable for a balance between £1,000 and £1,000,000 GBP, or the value of a salary, etc. It is recommended to use [[PriceSpecification]] Types to describe
MonetaryAmount.label: MonetaryAmount

MonetaryAmountDistribution.description: A statistical distribution of monetary amounts.
MonetaryAmountDistribution.label: MonetaryAmountDistribution

MoneyTransfer.description: The act of transferring money from one place to another place. This may occur electronically or physically.
MoneyTransfer.label: MoneyTransfer

MortgageLoan.description: A loan in which property or real estate is used as collateral. (A loan securitized against some real estate.)
MortgageLoan.label: MortgageLoan

Motel.description:
A motel.

See also the dedicated document on the use of schema.org for marking up hotels and other forms of accommodations.
Motel.label: Motel

Motorcycle.description: A motorcycle or motorbike is a single-track, two-wheeled motor vehicle.
Motorcycle.label: Motorcycle

MotorizedBicycle.description: A motorized bicycle is a bicycle with an attached motor used to power the vehicle, or to assist with pedaling.
MotorizedBicycle.label: MotorizedBicycle

Mountain.description: A mountain, like Mount Whitney or Mount Everest.
Mountain.label: Mountain

MoveAction.description:
The act of an agent relocating to a place.

Related actions:

* [[TransferAction]]: Unlike TransferAction, the subject of the move is a living Person or Organization rather than an inanimate object.
MoveAction.label: MoveAction

MovieClip.description: A short segment/part of a movie.
MovieClip.label: MovieClip

MovieSeries.description: A series of movies. Included movies can be indicated with the hasPart property.
MovieSeries.label: MovieSeries

Muscle.description: A muscle is an anatomical structure consisting of a contractile form of tissue that animals use to effect movement.
Muscle.label: Muscle

MusicAlbumProductionType.description: Classification of the album by its type of content: soundtrack, live album, studio album, etc.
MusicAlbumProductionType.label: MusicAlbumProductionType

MusicAlbumReleaseType.description: The kind of release which this album is: single, EP or album.
MusicAlbumReleaseType.label: MusicAlbumReleaseType

MusicGroup.description: A musical group, such as a band, an orchestra, or a choir. Can also be a solo musician.
MusicGroup.label: MusicGroup

MusicPlaylist.description: A collection of music tracks in playlist form.
MusicPlaylist.label: MusicPlaylist

MusicRecording.description: A music recording (track), usually a single song.
MusicRecording.label: MusicRecording

MusicRelease.description: A MusicRelease is a specific release of a music album.
MusicRelease.label: MusicRelease

MusicReleaseFormatType.description: Format of this release (the type of recording media used, i.e. compact disc, digital media, LP, etc.).
MusicReleaseFormatType.label: MusicReleaseFormatType

NLNonprofitType.description: NLNonprofitType: Non-profit organization type originating from the Netherlands.
NLNonprofitType.label: NLNonprofitType

Nerve.description: A common pathway for the electrochemical nerve impulses that are transmitted along each of the axons.
Nerve.label: Nerve

NewsArticle.description:
A NewsArticle is an article whose content reports news, or provides background context and supporting materials for understanding the news.

A more detailed overview of [schema.org News markup](/docs/news.html) is also available.
NewsArticle.label: NewsArticle

NewsMediaOrganization.description: A News/Media organization such as a newspaper or TV station.
NewsMediaOrganization.label: NewsMediaOrganization

Newspaper.description: A publication containing information about varied topics that are pertinent to general information, a geographic area, or a specific subject matter (i.e. business, culture, education). Often published daily.
Newspaper.label: Newspaper

NonprofitType.description: NonprofitType enumerates several kinds of official non-profit types of which a non-profit organization can be.
NonprofitType.label: NonprofitType

NoteDigitalDocument.description: A file containing a note, primarily for the author.
NoteDigitalDocument.label: NoteDigitalDocument

Number.description:
Data type: Number.

Usage guidelines:

* Use values from 0123456789 (Unicode 'DIGIT ZERO' (U+0030) to 'DIGIT NINE' (U+0039)) rather than superficially similar Unicode symbols.
* Use '.' (Unicode 'FULL STOP' (U+002E)) rather than ',' to indicate a decimal point. Avoid using these symbols as a re
Number.label: Number

Observation.description: Instances of the class [[Observation]] are used to specify observations about an entity at a particular time. The principal properties of an [[Observation]] are [[observationAbout]], [[measuredProperty]], [[statType]], [[value] and [[observationDate]]  and [[measuredProperty]]. Some but not all Obse
Observation.label: Observation

Occupation.description: A profession, may involve prolonged training and/or a formal qualification.
Occupation.label: Occupation

OccupationalTherapy.description: A treatment of people with physical, emotional, or social problems, using purposeful activity to help them overcome or learn to deal with their problems.
OccupationalTherapy.label: OccupationalTherapy

Offer.description: An offer to transfer some rights to an item or to provide a service — for example, an offer to sell tickets to an event, to rent the DVD of a movie, to stream a TV show over the internet, to repair a motorcycle, or to loan a book.\n\nNote: As the [[businessFunction]] property, which identifies the f
Offer.label: Offer

OfferCatalog.description: An OfferCatalog is an ItemList that contains related Offers and/or further OfferCatalogs that are offeredBy the same provider.
OfferCatalog.label: OfferCatalog

OfferForLease.description:
An [[OfferForLease]] in Schema.org represents an [[Offer]] to lease out something, i.e. an [[Offer]] whose
  [[businessFunction]] is [lease out](http://purl.org/goodrelations/v1#LeaseOut.). See [Good Relations](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/GoodRelations) for
  background on the underlying concepts.
OfferForLease.label: OfferForLease

OfferForPurchase.description:
An [[OfferForPurchase]] in Schema.org represents an [[Offer]] to sell something, i.e. an [[Offer]] whose
  [[businessFunction]] is [sell](http://purl.org/goodrelations/v1#Sell.). See [Good Relations](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/GoodRelations) for
  background on the underlying concepts.
OfferForPurchase.label: OfferForPurchase

OfferItemCondition.description: A list of possible conditions for the item.
OfferItemCondition.label: OfferItemCondition

OfferShippingDetails.description:
OfferShippingDetails represents information about shipping destinations.

Multiple of these entities can be used to represent different shipping rates for different destinations:

One entity for Alaska/Hawaii. A different one for continental US. A different one for all France.

Multiple of these ent
OfferShippingDetails.label: OfferShippingDetails

OnDemandEvent.description: A publication event, e.g. catch-up TV or radio podcast, during which a program is available on-demand.
OnDemandEvent.label: OnDemandEvent

OnlineBusiness.description: A particular online business, either standalone or the online part of a broader organization. Examples include an eCommerce site, an online travel booking site, an online learning site, an online logistics and shipping provider, an online (virtual) doctor, etc.
OnlineBusiness.label: OnlineBusiness

OpeningHoursSpecification.description:
A structured value providing information about the opening hours of a place or a certain service inside a place.


The place is __open__ if the [[opens]] property is specified, and __closed__ otherwise.

If the value for the [[closes]] property is less than the value for the [[opens]] property t
OpeningHoursSpecification.label: OpeningHoursSpecification

OperatingSystem.description: System software that manages computer hardware and software resources, and provides common services for computer programs.
OperatingSystem.label: OperatingSystem

OpinionNewsArticle.description: An [[OpinionNewsArticle]] is a [[NewsArticle]] that primarily expresses opinions rather than journalistic reporting of news and events. For example, a [[NewsArticle]] consisting of a column or [[Blog]]/[[BlogPosting]] entry in the Opinions section of a news publication.
OpinionNewsArticle.label: OpinionNewsArticle

Optician.description: A store that sells reading glasses and similar devices for improving vision.
Optician.label: Optician

Order.description: An order is a confirmation of a transaction (a receipt), which can contain multiple line items, each represented by an Offer that has been accepted by the customer.
Order.label: Order

OrderAction.description: An agent orders an object/product/service to be delivered/sent.
OrderAction.label: OrderAction

OrderItem.description: An order item is a line of an order. It includes the quantity and shipping details of a bought offer.
OrderItem.label: OrderItem

Organization.description: An organization such as a school, NGO, corporation, club, etc.
Organization.label: Organization

OrganizationRole.description: A subclass of Role used to describe roles within organizations.
OrganizationRole.label: OrganizationRole

OrganizeAction.description: The act of manipulating/administering/supervising/controlling one or more objects.
OrganizeAction.label: OrganizeAction

OwnershipInfo.description: A structured value providing information about when a certain organization or person owned a certain product.
OwnershipInfo.label: OwnershipInfo

PaintAction.description: The act of producing a painting, typically with paint and canvas as instruments.
PaintAction.label: PaintAction

PalliativeProcedure.description: A medical procedure intended primarily for palliative purposes, aimed at relieving the symptoms of an underlying health condition.
PalliativeProcedure.label: PalliativeProcedure

ParcelDelivery.description: The delivery of a parcel either via the postal service or a commercial service.
ParcelDelivery.label: ParcelDelivery

ParentAudience.description: A set of characteristics describing parents, who can be interested in viewing some content.
ParentAudience.label: ParentAudience

ParkingFacility.description: A parking lot or other parking facility.
ParkingFacility.label: ParkingFacility

PathologyTest.description: A medical test performed by a laboratory that typically involves examination of a tissue sample by a pathologist.
PathologyTest.label: PathologyTest

Patient.description: A patient is any person recipient of health care services.
Patient.label: Patient

PawnShop.description: A shop that will buy, or lend money against the security of, personal possessions.
PawnShop.label: PawnShop

PayAction.description: An agent pays a price to a participant.
PayAction.label: PayAction

PaymentCard.description: A payment method using a credit, debit, store or other card to associate the payment with an account.
PaymentCard.label: PaymentCard

PaymentChargeSpecification.description: The costs of settling the payment using a particular payment method.
PaymentChargeSpecification.label: PaymentChargeSpecification

PaymentMethod.description: A payment method is a standardized procedure for transferring the monetary amount for a purchase. Payment methods are characterized by the legal and technical structures used, and by the organization or group carrying out the transaction. The following legacy values should be accepted: \n\n* http://
PaymentMethod.label: PaymentMethod

PaymentMethodType.description: The type of payment method, only for generic payment types, specific forms of payments, like card payment should be expressed using subclasses of PaymentMethod.
PaymentMethodType.label: PaymentMethodType

PaymentService.description: A Service to transfer funds from a person or organization to a beneficiary person or organization.
PaymentService.label: PaymentService

PaymentStatusType.description: A specific payment status. For example, PaymentDue, PaymentComplete, etc.
PaymentStatusType.label: PaymentStatusType

PeopleAudience.description: A set of characteristics belonging to people, e.g. who compose an item's target audience.
PeopleAudience.label: PeopleAudience

PerformAction.description: The act of participating in performance arts.
PerformAction.label: PerformAction

PerformanceRole.description: A PerformanceRole is a Role that some entity places with regard to a theatrical performance, e.g. in a Movie, TVSeries etc.
PerformanceRole.label: PerformanceRole

PerformingArtsEvent.description:
Live performance Event of the performing arts (music, theatre, dance, acrobatics, spoken word), including performance art and performative sports (e.g. choreographed forms of martial arts, figure skating, competitive ballroom dancing).

Not
PerformingArtsEvent.label: PerformingArtsEvent

PerformingArtsTheater.description: A theater or other performing art center.
PerformingArtsTheater.label: PerformingArtsTheater

PerformingGroup.description: A performance group, such as a band, an orchestra, or a circus.
PerformingGroup.label: PerformingGroup

Periodical.description: A publication in any medium issued in successive parts bearing numerical or chronological designations and intended to continue indefinitely, such as a magazine, scholarly journal, or newspaper.\n\nSee also [blog post](https://blog.schema.org/2014/09/02/schema-org-support-for-bibliographic-relations
Periodical.label: Periodical

Permit.description: A permit issued by an organization, e.g. a parking pass.
Permit.label: Permit

Person.description: A person (alive, dead, undead, or fictional).
Person.label: Person

PhotographAction.description: The act of capturing still images of objects using a camera.
PhotographAction.label: PhotographAction

PhysicalActivity.description: Any bodily activity that enhances or maintains physical fitness and overall health and wellness. Includes activity that is part of daily living and routine, structured exercise, and exercise prescribed as part of a medical treatment or recovery plan.
PhysicalActivity.label: PhysicalActivity

PhysicalActivityCategory.description: Categories of physical activity, organized by physiologic classification.
PhysicalActivityCategory.label: PhysicalActivityCategory

PhysicalExam.description: A type of physical examination of a patient performed by a physician.
PhysicalExam.label: PhysicalExam

PhysicalTherapy.description: A process of progressive physical care and rehabilitation aimed at improving a health condition.
PhysicalTherapy.label: PhysicalTherapy

Physician.description: An individual physician or a physician's office considered as a [[MedicalOrganization]].
Physician.label: Physician

Place.description: Entities that have a somewhat fixed, physical extension.
Place.label: Place

PlaceOfWorship.description: Place of worship, such as a church, synagogue, or mosque.
PlaceOfWorship.label: PlaceOfWorship

PlanAction.description: The act of planning the execution of an event/task/action/reservation/plan to a future date.
PlanAction.label: PlanAction

Play.description: A play is a form of literature, usually consisting of dialogue between characters, intended for theatrical performance rather than just reading. Note: A performance of a Play would be a [[TheaterEvent]] or [[BroadcastEvent]] - the *Play* being the [[workPerformed]].
Play.label: Play

PlayAction.description:
The act of playing/exercising/training/performing for enjoyment, leisure, recreation, competition or exercise.

Related actions:

* [[ListenAction]]: Unlike ListenAction (which is under ConsumeAction), PlayAction refers to performing for an audience or at an event, rather than consuming music.
PlayAction.label: PlayAction

PlayGameAction.description: The act of playing a video game.
PlayGameAction.label: PlayGameAction

PodcastEpisode.description: A single episode of a podcast series.
PodcastEpisode.label: PodcastEpisode

PodcastSeason.description: A single season of a podcast. Many podcasts do not break down into separate seasons. In that case, PodcastSeries should be used.
PodcastSeason.label: PodcastSeason

PodcastSeries.description: A podcast is an episodic series of digital audio or video files which a user can download and listen to.
PodcastSeries.label: PodcastSeries

PostalCodeRangeSpecification.description: Indicates a range of postal codes, usually defined as the set of valid codes between [[postalCodeBegin]] and [[postalCodeEnd]], inclusively.
PostalCodeRangeSpecification.label: PostalCodeRangeSpecification

Poster.description: A large, usually printed placard, bill, or announcement, often illustrated, that is posted to advertise or publicize something.
Poster.label: Poster

PreOrderAction.description: An agent orders a (not yet released) object/product/service to be delivered/sent.
PreOrderAction.label: PreOrderAction

PrependAction.description: The act of inserting at the beginning if an ordered collection.
PrependAction.label: PrependAction

PresentationDigitalDocument.description: A file containing slides or used for a presentation.
PresentationDigitalDocument.label: PresentationDigitalDocument

PreventionIndication.description: An indication for preventing an underlying condition, symptom, etc.
PreventionIndication.label: PreventionIndication

PriceComponentTypeEnumeration.description: Enumerates different price components that together make up the total price for an offered product.
PriceComponentTypeEnumeration.label: PriceComponentTypeEnumeration

PriceSpecification.description: A structured value representing a price or price range. Typically, only the subclasses of this type are used for markup. It is recommended to use [[MonetaryAmount]] to describe independent amounts of money such as a salary, credit card limits, etc.
PriceSpecification.label: PriceSpecification

PriceTypeEnumeration.description: Enumerates different price types, for example list price, invoice price, and sale price.
PriceTypeEnumeration.label: PriceTypeEnumeration

Product.description: Any offered product or service. For example: a pair of shoes; a concert ticket; the rental of a car; a haircut; or an episode of a TV show streamed online.
Product.label: Product

ProductCollection.description: A set of products (either [[ProductGroup]]s or specific variants) that are listed together e.g. in an [[Offer]].
ProductCollection.label: ProductCollection

ProductGroup.description:
A ProductGroup represents a group of [[Product]]s that vary only in certain well-described ways, such as by [[size]], [[color]], [[material]] etc.

While a ProductGroup itself is not directly offered for sale, the various varying products that it represents can be. The ProductGroup serves as a proto
ProductGroup.label: ProductGroup

ProductModel.description: A datasheet or vendor specification of a product (in the sense of a prototypical description).
ProductModel.label: ProductModel

ProfessionalService.description:
Original definition: "provider of professional services."\n\nThe general [[ProfessionalService]] type for local businesses was deprecated due to confusion with [[Service]]. For reference, the types that it included were: [[Dentist]],
        [[AccountingService]], [[Attorney]], [[Notary]], as well a
ProfessionalService.label: ProfessionalService

ProgramMembership.description: Used to describe membership in a loyalty programs (e.g. "StarAliance"), traveler clubs (e.g. "AAA"), purchase clubs ("Safeway Club"), etc.

PsychologicalTreatment.description: A process of care relying upon counseling, dialogue and communication  aimed at improving a mental health condition without use of drugs.

PurchaseType.description: Enumerates a purchase type for an item.

Quiz.description: Quiz: A test of knowledge, skills and abilities.

QuoteAction.description: An agent quotes/estimates/appraises an object/product/service with a price at a location/store.

RVPark.description: A place offering space for "Recreational Vehicles", Caravans, mobile homes and the like.

RadiationTherapy.description: A process of care using radiation aimed at improving a health condition.
RadiationTherapy.label: RadiationTherapy

RadioBroadcastService.description: A delivery service through which radio content is provided via broadcast over the air or online.

RadioChannel.description: A unique instance of a radio BroadcastService on a CableOrSatelliteService lineup.

RadioClip.description: A short radio program or a segment/part of a radio program.

RadioEpisode.description: A radio episode which can be part of a series or season.

RadioSeason.description: Season dedicated to radio broadcast and associated online delivery.
RadioSeason.label: RadioSeason

RadioSeries.description: CreativeWorkSeries dedicated to radio broadcast and associated online delivery.
RadioSeries.label: RadioSeries

Rating.description: A rating is an evaluation on a numeric scale, such as 1 to 5 stars.
Rating.label: Rating

ReactAction.description: The act of responding instinctively and emotionally to an object, expressing a sentiment.
ReactAction.label: ReactAction

ReadAction.description: The act of consuming written content.

A [[RealEstateListing]] is a listing that describes one or more real-estate [[Offer]]s (whose [[businessFunction]] is typically to lease out, or to sell).
  The [[RealEstateListing]] type itself represents the overall listing, as manifested in some [[WebPage]].

The act of physically/electronically taking delivery of an object that has been transferred from an origin to a destination. Reciprocal of SendAction.

* [[TakeAction]]: Unlike TakeAction, ReceiveAction does not imply that the
ReceiveAction.label: ReceiveAction

Recommendation.label: Recommendation

ReportageNewsArticle.label: ReportageNewsArticle

Resort.label: Resort

Role.label: Role

ShippingRateSettings.label: ShippingRateSettings

TouristAttraction.label: TouristAttraction

TouristDestination.label: TouristDestination
